Class Tile

java.lang.Object
logic.board.Tile
All Implemented Interfaces:
Flammable
public class Tile extends Object implements Flammable
Represents a single discrete cell or coordinate space on the game board's grid. A tile holds information about its terrain type, its physical position, and the unit currently occupying it. It also implements the Flammable interface, allowing the terrain itself to catch fire, burn for a set duration, and spread fire to grounded units that step on it.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Tile(int r, int c)
    Constructs a new empty Tile at the specified coordinates with a default terrain type of TileType.PLAIN.
    Tile(int r, int c, TileType type)
    Constructs a new empty Tile at the specified coordinates with a specific terrain type.

  • Constructor Details

    • Tile

      public Tile(int r, int c)
      Constructs a new empty Tile at the specified coordinates with a default terrain type of TileType.PLAIN.
      Parameters:
      r - The row index.
      c - The column index.

    • Tile

      public Tile(int r, int c, TileType type)
      Constructs a new empty Tile at the specified coordinates with a specific terrain type.
      Parameters:
      r - The row index.
      c - The column index.
      type - The designated TileType for this tile.

  • Method Details

    • getImagePath

      public String getImagePath()
      Retrieves the file path for the image asset corresponding to this tile's current terrain type.
      Returns:
      A string representing the image path.

    • isOccupied

      public boolean isOccupied()
      Checks if a unit is currently stationed on this tile.
      Returns:
      true if an occupant exists; false if the tile is empty.

    • getOccupant

      public BaseUnit getOccupant()
      Retrieves the unit currently occupying this tile.
      Returns:
      The occupying BaseUnit, or null if unoccupied.

    • setOccupant

      public void setOccupant(BaseUnit unit)
      Sets the unit occupying this tile. This method also handles side effects of a unit arriving: if a burning, non-flying unit steps on the tile, it will ignite the tile. It also triggers the unit's positional passive skills.
      Parameters:
      unit - The BaseUnit entering the tile, or null to clear the tile.

    • getType

      public TileType getType()
      Retrieves the specific terrain type of this tile.
      Returns:
      The current TileType.

    • setType

      public void setType(TileType type)
      Changes the terrain type of this tile.
      Parameters:
      type - The new TileType to apply.

    • getMoveCost

      public int getMoveCost()
      Retrieves the stamina or movement cost required for a standard unit to enter this tile.
      Returns:
      The movement cost integer defined by the tile's terrain type.

    • isBlocking

      public boolean isBlocking()
      Checks if the tile intrinsically blocks movement (e.g., a mountain or wall).
      Returns:
      true if the terrain type is blocking; false otherwise.

    • hasBurnt

      public boolean hasBurnt()
      Checks if this tile has previously been subjected to fire.
      Returns:
      true if the tile has burnt or is currently burning; false otherwise.

    • isBurning

      public boolean isBurning()
      Checks if this tile is currently actively burning.
      Specified by:
      isBurning in interface Flammable
      Returns:
      true if the tile is on fire; false otherwise.

    • isFlammable

      public boolean isFlammable()
      Checks if this tile is capable of catching fire. A tile is flammable if its terrain type allows it and it hasn't already burnt.
      Specified by:
      isFlammable in interface Flammable
      Returns:
      true if the tile can ignite; false otherwise.

    • ignite

      public void ignite()
      Sets the tile on fire if it is flammable. Morphs the underlying TileType to a burnt variant (e.g., PLAIN to PLAIN_BURNT) and initializes the burning duration based on the terrain type.
      Specified by:
      ignite in interface Flammable

    • burn

      public void burn()
      Executes the recurring burning logic for this tile. Decrements the active burn duration and applies fire effects to any unit currently standing on it. Automatically extinguishes the fire when the duration expires.
      Specified by:
      burn in interface Flammable

    • extinguish

      public void extinguish()
      Forces the fire on this tile to go out.
      Specified by:
      extinguish in interface Flammable

    • canPlace

      public boolean canPlace(BaseUnit unit)
      Evaluates whether a specific unit can be legally placed on or moved into this tile. Verifies that the tile is not intrinsically blocking, is not already occupied, and adheres to the unit's movement rules (e.g., sailing units can only enter water).
      Parameters:
      unit - The BaseUnit attempting to enter the tile.
      Returns:
      true if placement is valid; false otherwise.

    • distanceTo

      public int distanceTo(Tile tile)
      Calculates the Manhattan distance (grid steps) between this tile and another tile.
      Parameters:
      tile - The target Tile.
      Returns:
      The integer distance between the two tiles.

    • getRow

      public int getRow()
      Retrieves the row index of this tile.
      Returns:
      The integer row coordinate.

    • getCol

      public int getCol()
      Retrieves the column index of this tile.
      Returns:
      The integer column coordinate.

    • getPosition

      public GridPoint getPosition()
      Retrieves the underlying grid coordinate object for this tile.
      Returns:
      The GridPoint representing this tile's location.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the tile's current state, useful for debugging.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      A descriptive string detailing the tile's position, type, occupant, and burning status.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Compares this tile to another object for equality. Two tiles are considered equal if they occupy the exact same grid coordinates.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - The object to compare with.
      Returns:
      true if the given object represents a tile at the same position; false otherwise.
